Title: PbrmiR397a regulates lignification during stone cell development in pear fruit

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: lignin polymerization in secondary cell wall / stone cell lignification
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms lignin polymer from monolignols
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: oxidizes monolignols to radicals that polymerize into lignin in the cell wall
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control step for lignification; functionally redundant with PbrLAC2 and PbrLAC18
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: secondary cell wall strengthening and stone cell formation; affects pear fruit texture/quality
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: pear fruit flesh stone cells; tobacco stem xylem/cell walls
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes lignin accumulation and stone cell lignification
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PbrLAC1 is highly expressed during early pear fruit development, matching lignin deposition timing. 2. It is a validated cleavage target of PbrmiR397a by 5'-RACE and luciferase assays. 3. Single antisense silencing caused only slight lignin reduction, but simultaneous silencing with PbrLAC2 and PbrLAC18 significantly reduced pear fruit lignin, indicating redundant positive roles in lignification.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: developmental expression profiling; subcellular localization; 5'-RACE target validation; luciferase reporter assay; transient antisense silencing in pear fruit; lignin staining and quantification
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: high at 21 and 35 DAF in pear fruit, then decreases rapidly
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: fruit development stage; transient agroinfiltration in pear fruit
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No significant association between PbrLAC1 genomic SNPs and stone cell content among 60 pear varieties; functional redundancy with PbrLAC2 and PbrLAC18
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: reduce stone cells/lignin by jointly suppressing multiple PbrLAC genes or by increasing upstream PbrmiR397a activity
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: single-gene suppression insufficient; direct developmental penalty in pear fruit not reported

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: lignin polymerization in secondary cell wall / stone cell lignification
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms lignin polymer from monolignols
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: oxidizes monolignols to radicals that polymerize into lignin in the cell wall
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control step for lignification; functionally redundant with PbrLAC1 and PbrLAC18
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: secondary cell wall strengthening and stone cell formation; affects pear fruit texture/quality
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: pear fruit flesh stone cells; tobacco stem xylem/cell walls
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes lignin accumulation and stone cell lignification
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PbrLAC2 is abundantly expressed in early pear fruit development when stone cells form. 2. It is a validated PbrmiR397a target by 5'-RACE and luciferase reporter assay. 3. Only combined silencing with PbrLAC1 and PbrLAC18 significantly reduced lignin, supporting functional redundancy in lignin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: developmental expression profiling; subcellular localization; 5'-RACE target validation; luciferase reporter assay; transient antisense silencing in pear fruit; lignin staining and quantification
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: high at 21 and 35 DAF in pear fruit, then decreases rapidly
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: fruit development stage; transient agroinfiltration in pear fruit
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No significant association between PbrLAC2 genomic SNPs and stone cell content among 60 pear varieties; redundancy with PbrLAC1 and PbrLAC18
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: target in multi-gene suppression strategies to reduce lignin and stone cells
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: single-gene suppression insufficient; direct developmental penalty in pear fruit not reported

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: lignin polymerization in secondary cell wall / stone cell lignification
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms lignin polymer from monolignols
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: oxidizes monolignols to radicals that polymerize into lignin in the cell wall
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control step for lignification; functionally redundant with PbrLAC1 and PbrLAC2
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: secondary cell wall strengthening and stone cell formation; affects pear fruit texture/quality
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: pear fruit flesh stone cells; tobacco stem xylem/cell walls
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes lignin accumulation and stone cell lignification
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PbrLAC18 is strongly expressed during early pear fruit development and localized to the cell wall. 2. It is a validated target of PbrmiR397a. 3. Simultaneous silencing with PbrLAC1 and PbrLAC2 significantly reduced fruit lignin, whereas single silencing had no significant effect, showing redundancy.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: developmental expression profiling; subcellular localization; 5'-RACE target validation; luciferase reporter assay; transient antisense silencing in pear fruit; lignin staining and quantification
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: high at 21 and 35 DAF in pear fruit, then decreases rapidly
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: fruit development stage; transient agroinfiltration in pear fruit
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No significant association between PbrLAC18 genomic SNPs and stone cell content among 60 pear varieties; redundancy with PbrLAC1 and PbrLAC2
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: target in stacked suppression/editing strategies for improving pear fruit texture
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: single-gene suppression insufficient; direct developmental penalty in pear fruit not reported

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: post-transcriptional cleavage and suppression of target mRNAs
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: lignin biosynthesis via regulation of laccase-mediated monolignol polymerization
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: PbrLAC1; PbrLAC2; PbrLAC3; PbrLAC15; PbrLAC18; PbrLAC20; additional PbrLACs; tobacco NbLAC1; NbLAC3; NbLAC10; NbLAC11; NbLAC12; NbLAC17
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: cleaves and represses multiple LAC transcripts, including PbrLAC1/2/18, thereby reducing lignification
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: major negative regulator of lignin accumulation and stone cell development by coordinately suppressing redundant laccase genes
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: secondary cell wall strengthening and stone cell formation; determinant of pear fruit quality
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: pear fruit stone cells; tobacco xylem secondary walls
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: reduces lignin content and stone cell number/content
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Transient overexpression of PbrmiR397a in pear fruit reduced lignin content and lignin staining. 2. PbrmiR397a directly targets and cleaves multiple PbrLAC and NbLAC transcripts, including core lignification genes. 3. Stable overexpression in tobacco decreased lignin content by 18%–29%, reduced vessel number and secondary wall thickness, but did not alter S/G ratio or biomass significantly. 4. A promoter SNP associated with low-stone-cell pear varieties creates an SA-responsive TCA element that increases promoter activity.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: expression profiling; 5'-RACE; dual-luciferase target assay in N. benthamiana; transient overexpression in pear fruit; stable overexpression in transgenic tobacco; lignin quantification; promoter-reporter assays; association analysis across 60 pear varieties
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: salicylic acid; promoter SNP #7 creating a TCA element
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: fruit developmental stage; salicylic acid treatment in protoplast assay
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: abundant at 21 DAF and decreases rapidly thereafter; higher in low-stone-cell pear varieties than in high-stone-cell varieties
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: possible indirect feedback indicated by up-regulation of POD and UGT72E after LAC repression, but not established for PbrmiR397a itself
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S constitutive promoter for overexpression; native 3.0-kb PbrmiR397a promoter tested in reporter assays
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: in tobacco, reduced lignin caused fewer vessel elements and thinner secondary walls, but no significant biomass penalty and no change in S/G ratio
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: valuable target for improving pear fruit quality by lowering stone cell/lignin content; promoter SNP can support marker-assisted breeding; overexpression may outperform single LAC manipulation due to multiplex target control
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Source species is pear, but functional effects were demonstrated in both pear fruit and transgenic tobacco; low-stone-cell varieties show higher PbrmiR397a expression linked to promoter polymorphism
